name: fixture-08-sales-engineer-enterprise-saas
description: Sr. Sales Engineer at a cybersecurity vendor applying to a Principal SE role at a data/AI platform. Strong pre-sales + technical credibility; the step up is managing SE team coverage and deal strategy at the VP-of-SE tier.

resume_data.name: Derek Nwosu
key_achievements: Supported $34M in closed ARR across 18 enterprise deals in FY 2024 — win rate 62% on qualified opportunities, $1.9M average deal size.
Built the company's first POV framework (success criteria, test plan, stakeholder map) adopted across 14 SEs and credited with reducing POV cycle time from 9 to 5 weeks.
Led the technical narrative on 3 flagship 7-figure deals including a $4.2M platform win against an entrenched competitor in financial services.

requirements: 7+ years of enterprise pre-sales experience, with $1M+ average deal size
Ability to lead technical strategy across 6-8 strategic accounts rather than a general territory
Deep technical fluency with modern data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks, or equivalent) and AI/ML use cases
Experience coaching newer SEs and influencing the SE playbook
Partnership with product and engineering on customer feedback loops
culture_cues: principal SEs set technical direction with the account team

letter_plan.opening_hook: Lead with the $34M ARR + 62% win rate — concrete evidence of pre-sales effectiveness — to establish credibility before the data/AI domain stretch.
body_points: $34M supported + $1.9M average deal size exceeds the JD's $1M+ threshold cleanly; frame this as the pre-sales depth that ports to the strategic-account motion Voltline runs.

POV framework adoption across 14 SEs is direct proof of the 'influence the SE playbook' expectation — this is principal-SE impact, not senior-SE activity. The 9→5 week POV cycle reduction is measurable outcome data for the application.

$4.2M financial-services flagship demonstrates the 7-figure technical-narrative muscle; address the data-platform / AI use-case specificity honestly — cyber and data/AI share the same enterprise buying motion, and Snowflake + Databricks are already in the cyber integration stack, so there's real technical continuity.
letter_plan.closing_strategy: Acknowledge the ML-specific use-case depth as the domain to build in the first 90 days. Close on the strategic-accounts appetite — the current role is territory-based and the strategic-accounts depth is exactly why this step makes sense, not just a title change.
